calendar.matters:
Notice Of Petition And Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award Against Current Construction
Off Calendar Jun-22-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. GERALD M. MURPHY, Esq. of Lubin Olson & Niewiadomski LLP (Tel: 415-981-0550) for petitioners;
Atty. KATHLEEN C. MILLER, Esq. of O'Brien Law, P.C. (Tel: 707-789-6500) for respondent Current Construction.

HEARING RE: PETITIONERS DENIS MCCARTHY AND SHEILA MCCARTHY's Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award Against Current Construction.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Petitioners Denis McCarthy and Sheila McCarthy's petition to confirm arbitration award is granted.

Respondent Current Construction fails to show that the award should be corrected pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1286.6. "Where a petition to confirm an award is at issue, the court is limited in its power to review the findings of the arbitrator and has narrow grounds upon which evaluate the arbitrator's award." (Cinel v. Christopher (2012) 203 Cal.App.4th 759, 769.)

The Court determines that there was no miscalculation of figures and that the arbitrator did not exceed his powers. It is evident that the arbitrator heard all of Respondent's arguments during the three-day arbitration, considered the evidence, provided his reasoning based on that evidence, and made his decision based thereon. (See Severtson v. Williams Constr. Co. (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 86, 93-94 ["no evident miscalculation can be discerned. An examination of the original award and accompanying letter would not reveal any miscalculation, but a deliberate choice rejecting certain items of evidence In providing that awards could be modified or corrected for 'evident miscalculation of figures,' we think our legislature had reference only to mathematical errors committed by arbitrators which would be patently clear to a reviewing court."]; Safari Assocs. v. Superior Ct. (2014) 231 Cal.App.4th 1400, 1413 [even a legally incorrect decision by an arbitrator "that explicitly contradict[s] the parties' agreement, is just that-a legally incorrect decision, which is not subject to correction by a trial court." (internal citations omitted)].)

Petitioners easily meet the relevant requirements of Code of Civil Procedure section 1285.4. Copies of the Construction Contract, which include provisions that require binding arbitration, are included with the Petition as Attachment 4(b). A copy of the Final Arbitration Award, that sets forth the factual findings supporting the arbitrator's decision, is attached as Attachment 8(c) to the Petition. Petitioners identify Marc Alan Fong as the arbitrator and the arbitration hearing took place in the City and County of San Francisco, California. Arbitrator Fong electronically served his order on May 5, 2022. The petition is therefore granted.

Counsel for petitioners to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
